description:
You are working as a monitoring specialist who has been asked to set-up a very small, test-bed security-alert workflow.

  1. A **sample application log** already exists at  
     /home/user/sample_app/logs/app.log

  2. Your job is to scan this log and create an **alert report** that
     • counts how many lines contain the string “ERROR” (case-sensitive)  
     • counts how many lines contain the string “CRITICAL” (case-sensitive)  
     • decides whether the overall scan result is “ALERT” or “OK”  
       – If (error_count + critical_count) > 10 ⇒ status = “ALERT”  
       – Otherwise                              ⇒ status = “OK”

  3. Create a directory (if it does not yet exist)  
     /home/user/alerts

  4. Inside that directory create ONE file named exactly  
     /home/user/alerts/security_alert.json

     The file **must contain valid JSON** with the following exact key
     names and order (whitespace and indentation are not tested):

     {
       "scan_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
       "scan_time": "HH:MM:SS",
       "error_count": <integer>,
       "critical_count": <integer>,
       "status": "OK" | "ALERT"
     }

     • scan_date / scan_time must be the local date & time at execution.  
     • error_count and critical_count must reflect the actual counts.  
     • status must strictly follow the rule in step 2.

  5. After the JSON file is written, print **one single line** to STDOUT
     in exactly this format (no extra spaces, no surrounding quotes):

     SUMMARY:<scan_date>:ERRORS=<error_count>:CRITICALS=<critical_count>:STATUS=<status>

     Example (values will differ):

     SUMMARY:2024-02-27:ERRORS=3:CRITICALS=0:STATUS=OK

  6. You do not need to set up any cron jobs or long-running daemons—
     just perform the one-off scan and create the alert artefacts.

  7. Use whatever standard shell utilities you like, but keep it to a
     reasonable number of commands (roughly 5-10 is fine).

  When you are finished, both the file and the console summary line will
  be checked automatically, so make sure the layout is **exactly** as
  specified.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing artefacts
  -->
  <initial_log_path>/home/user/sample_app/logs/app.log</initial_log_path>
  <initial_log_contents>
[2023-07-01 12:01:22] INFO User login successful
[2023-07-01 12:03:11] WARNING Disk usage at 81%
[2023-07-01 12:05:47] ERROR Failed to open /etc/passwd for writing
[2023-07-01 12:06:58] CRITICAL Unauthorized access attempt detected from 10.0.0.5
[2023-07-01 12:07:15] INFO Session terminated
[2023-07-01 12:10:02] ERROR SQL injection attempt blocked
[2023-07-01 12:12:33] ERROR Invalid user token
[2023-07-01 12:16:30] INFO Background job started
[2023-07-01 12:17:13] CRITICAL Buffer overflow in module auth.so
[2023-07-01 12:18:49] INFO Background job finished
[2023-07-01 12:22:11] ERROR Cannot establish TLS handshake
[2023-07-01 12:25:55] CRITICAL Root privilege escalation attempt
[2023-07-01 12:28:37] INFO User logout
[2023-07-01 12:30:04] ERROR Access denied for user 'guest'
[2023-07-01 12:33:27] WARNING Memory usage at 72%
[2023-07-01 12:35:42] CRITICAL Data tampering detected
[2023-07-01 12:37:00] INFO Cron job executed
[2023-07-01 12:40:18] ERROR Malformed input received
[2023-07-01 12:41:59] INFO Cron job completed
[2023-07-01 12:45:22] CRITICAL SELinux policy violation
  </initial_log_contents>

  <!--
    Ground-truth counts for verification
  -->
  <expected_error_count>6</expected_error_count>
  <expected_critical_count>5</expected_critical_count>
  <expected_status>ALERT</expected_status>

  <!--
    After execution we will verify:
      • /home/user/alerts/security_alert.json exists.
      • Its JSON includes "error_count": 6 and "critical_count": 5
        and "status": "ALERT".
      • The agent’s stdout contains one line that matches the exact
        tokenisation:
        SUMMARY:<any-date>:ERRORS=6:CRITICALS=5:STATUS=ALERT
  -->
name: task_000000_665388ba
